title: mariscus umbellatus var. evolutior
intro: Mariscus umbellatus var. evolutior, also known as the greater celandine, is a plant species belonging to the marigold family and is primarily found in Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Mariscus umbellatus var. evolutior include its ability to treat liver and gallbladder issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and ointments, and is often used in combination with other herbs for enhanced medicinal effects.
